One of the most critical videos that you can watch to your entire operations, whether you choose to in source or whether you choose to outsource your team and how you're going to handle everything inside of your business. The one thing that can make everything so much easier. We're going to learn inside of the products and services category, which is phase three. This video is called the project manager. You have to understand that in business, you have a lot that's going to go on. You have a lot of cycles that are going to start. You have a lot of people that are going to need a lot of things done. More clients that you have, more individual tasks that are going to rack up on talk to themselves. And you're just going to be overwhelmed until you get one critical position or you're smart and you're going to business with the one critical position already hired. Some people in business like to say they need an assistant. Some people think they need, you know, whatever else they think they need or whatever they want to justify for anything, what you need as a digital agent, so it is this project manager. Okay. If I could go back to the day that I started my business up, I would tell you that instead of a graphic designer and a salesperson, I would wipe out the salesperson and I instead would put a project manager and a graphic designer because alongside of myself, who was good at marketing automation and was good at creating funnels and landing pages and the advertisements we need to accomplish as a digital agency to start, maybe you're like me and you have that same skill. You might want to once again get a graphic designer and a project manager. If you're already in business or wherever you're at and you're thinking about putting this thing together, the one thing, once again, you have to look for as a project manager. I'm going to describe this person to you in detail because that's how critical this is. This individual, their viewpoint on life needs to be enthusiastic. They're love objections. They love obstacles. They love opportunity. They love overcoming things. They get accomplishment and fulfillment from completing tasks and cycles of actions. They can duplicate quickly, meaning if you tell them something to do, they have the ability to rapidly input that into your business so it gets done quickly. They also have the ability to manage your team and manage staff or manage the people that you're outsourcing business to and doing them in a stress-free way. They also need to have the ability to communicate upward, meaning they need to talk to you only about what's critical to you as an executive. You don't need to know everything in the business. You need to know specifically what relates to you getting a result or your clients getting a result or anything that needs your attention specifically. A lot of what a project manager has done for me has given me a stress-free environment and an ability for me to operate, go out and sell, and do whatever I feel I need to do to work on the business instead of being stuck in the business. This person hands down. Hands down is so necessary to just running any operation. I don't care what business I would go to in the future. I would always have some type of project manager on scene. Now, depending on what kind of agency you're going to run, it's going to be dependent on what you might want to pay this individual. So, depending on where you're at in business, if you're just starting up, one of the greatest things that you can do is you can look for interns. Now, I had a guy named David Meltzer who's a mentor of mine and also a cousin of my girlfriend and their family. Now, David Meltzer told me this about interns. He said, one third of all, we'll be shit. One third of what you're going to train, you're going to spend a lot of time on, they'll be mediocre. And one third of them are going to be superstars to stick with your business for life. He personally, instead of going out and finding interns in different businesses, I've taken the logic of what an intern is, which is an individual looking for an entry-level position at a low paying rate in order to acquire experience or mentorship. So, I give people obviously a ton of mentorship inside of the marketplace. You can imagine the amount of mentorship that I provide when somebody's inside of my business. My project manager, the role that this person builds currently in my business, her name is Casey. She's one of the best people I've ever had in my operation. She's secretly, meaning she doesn't seek it openly, but every time that I give her any kind of advice, counsel or mentorship, eats it up, loves it, consumes it, applies it. I see the results that she has from applying the advice that I give her, but she absolutely loves accomplishing things. I give her so many things and she eats it up. She thoroughly enjoys doing everything in my business. She loves it. That's what she absolutely loves. I call her up and say, hey, I need this done on this date and this time. She says, absolutely no problem. I'll keep you updated. I get texts about every cycle going on whenever I have requested it. This individual gets paid $4,000 a month in a business that this very month is going to do about $110,000 in revenue. Now, take that in. $4,000 a month is going towards this role. This role once again, just as depicted by myself to you, runs my business essentially. Why am I only giving you that amount of money? There's a bonus on top of that. There's an incentive, dependents on the amount of projects and the timetables that she can accomplish said projects dictates a bonus that could or may not be given to her dependent once again on result. Anybody in my business, I don't care who they are, what they do or how valuable they are to me. They have some kind of base rate paid just to accomplish bills and give them a stress-free kind of mindset for like a come-to-no-die factor, but all of them have an incentive to do more than what they're paid for based on results in business. This individual should be somehow bonus based on you giving them timetables to accomplish things in and little bonuses of dollar values or rewards in some kind in order to have them kind of incentivized. Now, how do you find this individual? I would go back to some of the tools and resources that I've given you in the tools and resources video that you can find in phase number one, which is the clarity and understanding phase, specifically under ones again, the tools and resources force. You can find this individual through a ZIP recruiter post. I found my project manager through my CO of she went out of a way to find a friend that lived in her neighborhood who was currently looking for work who just got back from the homeless after she'd split up from a relationship or bad business. She came in looking for comfortability wanting to work for somebody, but she wanted to do it at a high level as a manager or as an executive. The perfect kind of mindset and viewpoint to come into your business. Another detail I'll give you about this individual is they should work on statistics. I think this person should always be seeking out different metrics inside of the business in which they hold themselves accountable to and in which they are holding others accountable to. I personally pay for executive coaching and business consulting inside of my business for myself for months of my operations. However, I just recently expanded that into my project manager also getting that executive education for the plan of giving her some kind of bonus or of award and said I'm in order to promote herself to an executive. That being said, I can't state it enough just wrap up and summarize this. It's going to be the key position in your company and organization because this individual, this is you up here. As you go throughout your business and you're out in the world closing clients and getting deals and all that fun stuff, imagine this. You have one person that you go to who's your project manager and that person then goes and talks to your whole team. So they talk to your graphics team. They talk to that video guy that you have. They talk to your web development team. They out over here. They're communicating to your accounting team or like billing and over here they're talking to the legal and they're also doing all of your organizations. They're doing CRM and database management. Just imagine that everything that you didn't have here you would be doing yourself or even worse you would be managing yourself and you have employees in each of these areas. Project manager can literally just be you. Find somebody who is just like you. Duplicate yourself. Look for this individual because this is going to be the most critical role that you can have in your entire business. They manage it all. I'm going to give you in the comment section below, right below on that download button there's going to be something that says PM hat. Now in business, whenever you're looking to hire people and we'll talk about this in the team building section, you want to create what's called a hat which is a listed document if you will of roles, responsibilities, bowl of points, summarizations, viewpoints of what this person is going to be and who they need to be in order to join your organization. Now inside of the project manager hat that I provide below, you're going to find the exact details of what you need and what the ideal scene for this person is in order to go out of your way to find the perfect role for this position. Now, that being said, if you have any questions on that or if you need help implementing the hat or turning the hat into a job posting, I encourage you shoot an email and I'll help you out. This has been the project manager course inside of the products and services section of phase three.
